warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.limit: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants in food and feed commodities to protect human health, based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017)
rules.notes: This Code of Practice provides guidance for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxin contamination specifically in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.notes: The database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, and process hygiene criteria for foods placed on the market.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.notes: As a general labelling regulation for prepackaged foods, GSO 9:2022 is expected to include requirements for the declaration of allergens. The specific list of allergens and declaration rules must be verified within the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.notes: This technical regulation sets out the general requirements for the labelling of all prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details regarding mandatory information, format, and language must be verified within the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.notes: GSO 9:2022, as a general labelling regulation, is expected to cover nutritional information, including the declaration of salt or sodium content. Specific requirements for declaration and any potential limits or guidelines must be verified within the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.foodCategory: Halal Food (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods if marketed as Halal)
rules.parameter: Halal compliance for ingredients and additives
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1: General Requirements
rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for Halal food, which includes criteria for ingredients and processing, indirectly impacting additive choices.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 Gulf Technical Regulation --> HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged food stuffs (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 Gulf Technical Regulation --> Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption and food ingredients used in food industry (including seasoning, snack food, processed foods)
rules.notes: This technical regulation sets microbiological limits for various food products and ingredients. Specific limits are detailed within the full standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Mandatory allergen declaration requirements for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific contaminant limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: General labelling requirements and specific warnings for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific salt/sodium content limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan' (BPOM Regulations) under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan; Peraturan Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan

rules.parameter: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
rules.limit: Prohibited unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health. Verification required.
rules.notes: Specific additives and their permitted uses must be verified against MHLW specifications.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives... must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.limit: Must conform to Japan's Specifications and Standards for Food Additives. Verification required.
rules.notes: Specific specifications and standards for food additives must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Chapter V Japan's Specifications and Standards for Food Additives

rules.parameter: Ingredients of food or additives, production/processing/use/cooking/preservation methods
rules.limit: Must conform to criteria for methods and standards for ingredients established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.
rules.notes: Specific criteria and standards for food and additives must be verified against MHLW publications, such as 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals
rules.limit: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health, unless specific residual limits are established. Verification required.
rules.notes: Specific residual limits for agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals must be verified against MHLW specifications (Positive List System).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported... or sold

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances, foreign substances
rules.limit: Prohibited if containing or bearing toxic or harmful substances (unless MHLW specifies harmless) or likely to cause harm due to contamination or foreign substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; ... those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances

rules.limit: Subject to labelling and advertising regulations. Verification required.
rules.notes: Specific labelling and advertising requirements must be verified against Chapter IV of the Food Sanitation Act and related ordinances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Chapter IV Labeling and Advertising

rules.limit: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens and likely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.notes: When two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to determine permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Refer to Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.limit: Must be assessed and permitted by SFA
rules.notes: Only SFA-assessed and permitted food additives are allowed for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.notes: Food businesses must use the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to check if additives are allowed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food products containing contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and Food Derived from Meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of these compounds.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol [...] hexoestrol [...] dienoestrol

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Various limits are imposed on different types of contaminants (e.g., microbiological, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents). Refer to relevant SFA documents for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food.

rules.parameter: Veterinary Drug Residues (other than prohibited compounds)
rules.limit: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) apply; verification required
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of MRLs for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, available for download from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels
rules.notes: All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products for sale in Singapore must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.notes: Prepacked food products must comply with general labelling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Your food or food product must also comply with the labelling requirements.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Microbiological standards apply to ready-to-eat food. Refer to SFA documents for specific limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food is not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements are detailed in the referenced regulation, applicable to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Recommended labeling if these allergens are present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling is required if these allergens are present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory declaration is required if sulphite as residue sulphur dioxide is present at 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard, applicable to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of cross-contamination in food products, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims are detailed in the referenced regulation, applicable to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.notes: Specific regulations for food additive usage must be verified from relevant schedules/databases.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.notes: Specific limits for contaminants must be verified from relevant standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.notes: Draft ASEAN Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for toxic substances are under consideration for 2026. Compliance with these limits will be required.
rules.evidenceQuote: (ร่าง) ค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs) ที่ผ่านการพิจารณาเพิ่มเติมในปี 2026

rules.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.notes: Specific limits for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues must be verified from relevant standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Public Health Notification (No. 469) B.E. 2569
rules.notes: Specific regulations apply to food in hermetically sealed containers, as per Notification No. 469. This may include processing, quality, and labeling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: ประกาศกระทรวงสาธารณสุข (ฉบับที่ 469) พ.ศ. 2569 ออกตามความในพระราชบัญญัติอาหาร พ.ศ. 2522 เรื่อง อาหารในภาชนะบรรจุที่ปิดสนิท

rules.notes: Food products must adhere to general food labeling and specific nutrition labeling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.notes: Specific limits for pathogenic microorganisms must be verified from relevant standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively) or evaluated as Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*. Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food are not permitted.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.notes: These nine foods are identified by law as major food allergens in the U.S. The addition of sesame was effective January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level for 3-MCPD in acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.notes: This is an Action Level for total aflatoxins in general foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: This is an Action Level for total aflatoxins in peanuts and peanut products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb

rules.notes: Contaminant levels, including tolerances, action levels, and guidance levels, are published in various guidance documents and regulations. Users must refer to the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool for a consolidated list.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.notes: The FDA has a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 08/12/2020).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.limit: Not required by law, must be truthful and not misleading
rules.notes: Advisory statements like 'may contain [allergen]' are not legally required and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been implemented.
rules.evidenceQuote: Consumers may also see advisory statements such as “may contain [allergen] or “produced in a facility that also uses [allergen].” Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.notes: Facilities must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. For example, the FDA requires facilities to put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact between products that contain allergens and those that are not supposed to contain them and to ensure that the products are accurately labeled with respect to allergens.

rules.notes: Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: Foods or ingredients containing a 'major food allergen' must be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.parameter: Method of major food allergen declaration
rules.notes: The allergen's food source must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement.
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” and “whey (milk)” — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement. Example: “Contains wheat, milk, and soy.”

rules.parameter: Specific declaration for tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
rules.notes: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.notes: Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of 10 parts per million or more total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
